app.name: ParseKit
app.tagline: ParseKit — a toolkit for parsing documents.

config.title: Configuration
config.ocrWorkersHint: Scanned PDFs use more CPU — try 1–2 workers for large OCR batches.

onboarding.title: Get started
onboarding.lead: ParseKit runs from your menu bar. Complete these steps for your first batch.
onboarding.installHint: Drag ParseKit to Applications (not Desktop), then quit and reopen from Applications. If macOS blocks launch, right-click → Open once.
onboarding.stepOutput: Pick where Markdown files are saved
onboarding.stepFiles: Drop files or a folder below
onboarding.trayHint: Tip: Click the ParseKit icon in the menu bar (top-right) anytime to reopen this panel.

deps.title: Optional converters
deps.hint: PDF parsing works without these. Install only if you parse Office docs or images.
deps.missingNote: Missing tools only affect matching file types — other formats still parse.

update.installFailed: Update failed. Try again or download from GitHub.
update.settingsHint: ParseKit checks GitHub Releases on launch. You can also check manually here.
update.checkFailed: Could not check for updates. Try again when online.

gatekeeper.title: First launch blocked?
gatekeeper.hint: After dragging ParseKit to Applications, macOS may block unsigned apps. Run this in Terminal once, or use Privacy & Security → Open Anyway.
gatekeeper.copyFailed: Could not copy to clipboard. Try again.

dropzone.title: Drag & drop files or folders here

history.title: History
history.empty: No batches yet. Run a parse to see history here.

errors.addFiles: Add files or a folder to parse.
errors.batchInterrupted: Batch stopped before this file finished.
errors.engineStalled: Parse engine stopped responding. Try again with fewer parallel workers.

settings.title: Settings
settings.appLanguageHint: Language for buttons, labels, and menus in ParseKit. Does not change your exported files.
settings.ocrLanguageHint: Language of text inside scanned PDFs and images you parse. Used only when OCR is on. Output format (TXT/MD/JSON) is separate; exported content keeps the document language.
settings.appearanceHint: Choose how ParseKit looks on your Mac.
settings.workersHint: How many files to parse at once. Higher is faster on large batches; lower uses less CPU.
settings.launchAtLoginHint: Start ParseKit automatically when you sign in to your Mac.
settings.finderHint: Right-click files in Finder → Quick Actions → Parse to Markdown with ParseKit. If an output folder is set in ParseKit, files parse there silently; otherwise ParseKit opens with the files loaded.
settings.finderInstalled: Quick Action installed. Enable it in System Settings → Keyboard → Keyboard Shortcuts → Services if needed.
settings.finderInstallFailed: Could not install Finder Quick Action.
settings.aboutPrivacy: Your files are read and written only on this Mac. When OCR is on, recognition runs with on-device Tesseract (language data may download once on first use).
settings.aboutDescription: ParseKit — a toolkit for parsing documents.
settings.aboutFormats: PDF, Office, spreadsheets, images → Markdown, text, or JSON.
settings.aboutLicenseNote: ParseKit uses LiteParse v2 by LlamaIndex, licensed under the Apache License 2.0.
